sections.title: The Basics
points: A joint is a place in the body where two or more bones meet.
Joints provide flexibility, allowing the body to bend and stretch.
Ligaments: Strong fibers that hold bones together at a joint.
Tendons: Tough tissues that attach muscles to the bones.

sections.title: Three Classes of Joints
points: 1. Immovable (Fixed) Joints: Allow no movement. Found in the Skull (Sutures).
2. Slightly Movable Joints: Allow limited movement. Found between the Vertebrae (Spine).
3. Freely Movable (Synovial) Joints: Allow a wide range of motion. Includes Hinge and Ball-and-Socket joints.

sections.title: The Lubrication Team (Synovial Joint)
points: Synovial Fluid: A thick, slippery liquid that lubricates the joint to reduce friction.
Synovial Membrane: The lining that produces and contains the synovial fluid.
Articular Cartilage: A smooth layer on bone ends that acts as a shock absorber and prevents bones from wearing out.

sections.text: Friction is the enemy! Without synovial fluid and cartilage, bones would grind together, creating heat and pain that leads to bone damage.

cards.mnemonic: Ligaments are like rubber bands for your bones!
Tendons are like strong ropes that pull the bone!

cards.fact: When a bone moves out of its normal position at a joint.
